*Trying to lose weight without giving up idli*? 🙌

This no-rice, high protein idli is light on the stomach, high on protein & keeps cravings away 💪✨.

Healthy eating can still be comforting 🌿.

*INGREDIENTS*
• Urad dal – 1 cup
• Moong dal – ½ cup
• Chana dal – ½ cup
• Lotus seeds (Makhana) – 1 cup
• Poha (Aval) – ½ cup (soaked, optional)
• Salt – as needed.

👩‍🍳 *PREPARATION*
1️⃣ Soak urad dal, moong dal, chana dal & makhana for 3–4 hours.
2️⃣ Grind everything along with soaked poha into a thick, smooth batter.
3️⃣ Add salt, mix well & ferment overnight.
4️⃣ Next day, mix gently & pour into idli moulds.
5️⃣ Steam for 10–12 minutes until soft & fluffy.
✨ Protein-rich, light & easy to digest.

🥗 *SERVING NOTE* 
Each medium idli provides 4–4.5 g protein
Enjoy with coconut chutney or sambar

The History of Idli 🍽️ and how it quietly became a carb-heavy food.

Mighty cholas might have brought these steamed rice cakes from heir trade routes with south-east asia back to South India and this Idli was born!

Idli did not start with rice. The earliest versions were protein-dominant, made largely from urad dal — slow-digesting, filling, and gut-friendly.

Over time, taste, cost, and convenience changed the recipe.
Rice took over because it was cheaper and gave volume, not because it was better nutrition.

Here’s the modern problem ⚠️ 
Most idlis today use a 3:1 rice-to-dal ratio. That means higher carbs,
very little protein, and faster blood sugar spikes.

Why this matters 🧠 
A protein-poor breakfast leaves you hungry, low on energy,
and craving food within hours.

How to fix idli without giving it up 💪 
Increase the dal ratio (2:1 or even 1:1). Add soybeans or paneer to the batter. Make idli work for your metabolism 

Same tradition. Smarter plate.

Your health should be in your control

Udaka - a wholesom, nutritious and flavourful no cook rasam/broth from the North Karnataka culinary world, which can be savoured as a soup or as an accompaniment with mudde, rice.

Uduka -
- Roast 1-2 oil smeared brinjals over flame, let it cool and peel.
- Fry 8-10 green chillies  and 1-2 tomatoes separately in a tspn of oil and let it cool
- Grinding - a cup of fresh coconut, 15-20 garlic cloves, 1 tspn of jeera,  roasted chillies and grind coarsely.
- To this add roasted tomato and 12-15 strands of coriander leaves and pulse.
- Add half of this mixture to the broth/bele kattu.
- To the remaining masala, add roasted and peeled brinjal and pulse for 2-3 seconds.
- Add the masala to the broth, salt to taste and some lemon juice and mix well.

If you’re searching for the best product for hair regrowth or the best hair growth serum, this is the routine I use and talk about all the time. It’s a straightforward approach using hair growth products like a hair growth serum (aka hair serum for hair growth) and a booster step—no guesswork. Comment HAIR BOMB and I’ll send the link for info

Opt for hair colours that are Ammonia, PPD, PTD, Hydrogen peroxide free as these ingredients can cause dryness, irritation and reactions on the scalp. Long term pigmentation concerns all over the face and neck can be triggered with long term use of hair dyes. 

L’Oréal Excellence Crème contains ammonia and PPD which can cause dryness and scalp sensitivity with repeated use.

Revlon ColorSilk is ammonia-free but still relies on oxidative dyes that can weaken hair over time. Because it is short contact the chances of reaction can come down. But still patch test is mandatory.

Kerastem offers better conditioning agents and gentler pigments, making it relatively less damaging.

Vegetal Bio Colour uses plant-based dyes but lacks strong longevity, leading to faster colour fade.

Indus Valley avoids harsh chemicals and supports hair health while providing decent grey coverage. 

Cuticolour uses advanced, low-damage dye technology with good colour payoff and hair-friendly formulation.

Boutique herb colour has inconsistent pigment quality and poor grey coverage with higher risk of irritation.

BBlunt prioritises cosmetic colour results but uses chemical dyes that can dry out hair shafts.

Paradyes uses semi-permanent, conditioning pigments. No ammonia and uses no bleach technique which protects the hair shaft from drying out. 

Streax gel dyes often contain higher ammonia levels and can be harsh on the scalp and hair fiber. Pigment is also too deep. Can stain the skin. 

Deyga relies on natural ingredients that are gentler on hair and scalp with balanced colour results.

Vegetal Safe Colour provides plant-based colouring with good scalp tolerance and acceptable coverage.

Godrej and Indica use strong chemical formulations that are more likely to cause dryness, damage, and sensitivity with frequent use. There is higher chance of long term pigmentation as well. 

Note : All the high rated dyes are with no ammonia, PPD and PTD

Bhog'er Khichuri ভোগের খিচুড়ি 😋 recipe 👇

This warm, veggie loaded, rice, and lentil porridge is often served as Bengali puja prasad. Bhog means prasad. Hence, bhoger khichuri 

Deets(for 4-5):
---
150g Gobindobhog rice 
150g moong dal
Salt to taste
1 tsp sugar
½ tbsp ghee
¼ tsp garam masala
Mustard oil for cooking 

Vegetables:
110g cauliflower florets 
70g carrots, diced 
60g potatoes, diced
70g tomato, chopped 
20g ginger, grated
2 large green chillies, slitted 
50g peas (frozen or fresh)

Dry spices:
1 tsp turmeric 
1¼ tsp cumin powder 
¾ tsp red chilli powder 
 
Spices to temper:
1 tsp radhuni
2 dry red chillies 
2 bay leaves 

 - Dry roast dal on medium heat, stirring constantly, until pinkish. Then wash the dal with plenty of water.
- Heat 4 tbsp mustard oil in a kadai. Fry cauliflower until golden and set it aside.
- Fry potatoes and carrots in the remaining oil and set them aside as well.
- Now, temper bay leaves, dry red chillies, and radhuni in the remaining oil (add a few tbsp of oil if needed)
- Add tomatoes and fry for 2 mins. 
- Add grated ginger and fry for 30 secs.
- Add washed rice and stir-fry for 3-4 mins on medium heat. 
- Add the dal and stir-fry for a minute.
- Add the dry spices and mix well.
- Add 5-6 cups of water followed by salt and sugar. Mix and cover the pot. Cook on medium to high heat, stirring frequently until rice and dal is cooked 80%. Add more water if it gets too tight.
- Now add the fried vegetables, peas, green chillies and mix. 
- Cook on low to medium heat until everything is well done. Khichuri gets thicker and tighter as it cools down. So, keep it a little runny as it finishes cooking. 
- When everything is well cooked, add ghee and garam masala and mix. 
- Switch off the heat and keep the pot covered until needed.

Kya aap jaante hain?
• Siddiqui: Jiska matlab hai “Truthful,” ye title pehle Khalifa Hazrat Abu Bakr se juda hai.  
• Khan: Ye asal mein ek title tha Rulers aur Leaders ke liye.  
• Syed: Iska matlab “Lord” ya “Master” hai, jo Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) ki family lineage (Fatima & Ali) se juda hai.  
• Qureshi: Direct connection Makkah ke Quraish tribe se.  
• Ansari: “Helpers” yaani woh log jinhone Hijrat ke waqt madad ki.  
• Ali: Jiska matlab hai “High” ya “Champion.”

Tretinoin does not directly grow hair,
but it can enhance the effectiveness of minoxidil when used correctly.

🔬 What science says:
• Tretinoin thins the stratum corneum, improving drug penetration
• Research shows it can increase minoxidil absorption by 2–3×
• Helps hair follicles shift from the telogen (resting) phase to the anagen (growth) phase

📚 Research evidence:
• Bazzano et al., Journal of the American Academy of Dermatology — tretinoin improved responsiveness to topical minoxidil
• Olsen et al., Dermatologic Therapy — tretinoin + minoxidil combination showed better results, especially in non-responders

🧴 Medical usage (strictly doctor-guided):
• Low-strength tretinoin (0.01%–0.025%)
• Applied only on selected scalp areas
• Usually used at night
• Minoxidil applied separately (morning or alternate timing)

⚠️ Possible risks:
• Irritation, redness, itching, dryness, scaling
• Wrong concentration or frequency can worsen hair fall

🚫 IMPORTANT DISCLAIMER:
• This content is for educational purposes only
• ❌ Do not use tretinoin without a doctor’s prescription
• Tretinoin is a prescription drug and not suitable for everyone

👉 Final takeaway:
Tretinoin is a supporting agent, not a standalone solution.
Minoxidil remains the main treatment — and the combination is effective only when prescribed and monitored by a doctor#health #skincare #hair

**Dudde Upkari | Konkani Style Pumpkin Stir Fry 🎃**

A simple, comforting Konkani dish made with pumpkin, coconut, and mild spices 🤍 Perfect with rice and dal.

**Ingredients:**
• Pumpkin (dudde)
• Coconut oil
• Mustard seeds
• Urad dal
• Green chillies
• Curry leaves
• Hing (asafoetida) powder
• Salt
• Jaggery
• Grated coconut

**Method:**

1. Remove the seeds from the pumpkin, chop into pieces, and wash well.
2. Heat coconut oil in a pan. Add mustard seeds and let them splutter.
3. Add urad dal and sauté till lightly golden.
4. Add curry leaves, green chillies, and a pinch of hing powder.
5. Add chopped pumpkin, salt, and jaggery.
6. Add water as needed – less for dry upkari, more for a slightly thick consistency.
7. Cover and cook until the pumpkin is soft and cooked well.
8. Add grated coconut, mix gently, and cook for 2 more minutes.
9. Turn off heat and serve hot.

Simple • Traditional • Comfort food 💛

If you’re in Palakkad,Kerala — DON’T MISS THIS SPOT!

📍Sree Saraswathy Tea Stall (also known as The Ramassery Idli Kada),30 mins drive from Palakkad
🧑‍🍳Idly set-30₹ 

Claims to be around 200 years old now, represent the fifth generation of the family continuing this Business.

Not your usual idly — Ramassery Idli is soft, flat, and sits beautifully between an idly and a dosa in texture. Made by a few Mudaliar families who migrated from Tamil Nadu generations ago, it still follows a secret, time-honoured batter method. The batter is steamed on cloth over a flat clay sieve (chatti/kuzhi), allowing steam to pass directly through for that melt-soft finish. Served with coconut chutney, tomato chutney, sambar, and spicy podi, this humble village breakfast remains one of Kerala’s most iconic food traditions.

👉Normal idly in TN - but Why it became so familiar in Kerala :- (One local legend credits a woman named Chitori Ammal, a member of a weaving family migrant of tn, as the first person to make the iconic idli & its longer shelf life and ease of carrying made them suitable for sale, and they eventually gained popularity, putting the village on the map)

A quiet reminder that some classics don’t need reinvention — just quality & History!

Sananga is a traditional Amazonian eye medicine made from the roots and bark of the Tabernaemontana plant, used for generations by Indigenous tribes of the Amazon.

It’s known not just for supporting physical eye health, but also for clearing energetic blockages, sharpening perception, and helping release emotional and mental stagnation. Many people work with sananga for grounding, focus, presence, and deeper inner clarity.

The sensation can be intense — a strong burning that passes — and that intensity is part of the medicine. It’s often described as a reset: physically, energetically, and emotionally.

This is sacred plant medicine, not a trend. It’s approached with respect, intention, and awareness, honoring the Indigenous wisdom it comes from.

If you’re curious, always do your own research, listen to your body, and work with trusted, ethical sources.

We have installed humidifier, Exhaust fan and temperature meter today

2. Exhaust fan is used to remove inside air and CO2 released by mushrooms outside so that fresh air can refill the room.
3. Temperature meter (2) was installed, one in light room another one in dark room to keep a track on temperature inside the farm.

✨Labra - saraswati puja special bengali style mixed veg✨
Recipe 👇
2 potatoes sliced 
100 gms pumpkin chopped 
4-5 broad beans (seem) chopped
1 brinjal cubed 
1 raw banana chopped
100 gms pui saag ( malabar leaves)
1 tspn paanchporan 
2-3 dry red chillies 
1 tspn grated ginger 
Salt to taste 
1 tspn tumeric powder 
1-2 tspn garma masala 
2 tbspn mustard oil 
📍Heat oil, add in the paanchporan, red chillies, with potatoes, pumpkin & broad beans. Saute for 10 mins 
📍Add in the raw banana with the brinjals. Cover & saute for another 10 mins 
📍last add the pui saag, along with salt & tumeric. Cover & cook for another 10-15 on low heat. 
Sprinkle garam masala with serve hot with bhog khichuri.

आज हम बना रहे हैं राजस्थानी स्टाइल मेथी दाना की सब्ज़ी, जो अपने देसी स्वाद और खुशबू के लिए पूरे राजस्थान में बहुत पसंद की जाती है।
इस सब्ज़ी को खास तरीके से बनाया जाता है ताकि मेथी दाने की कड़वाहट दूर हो जाए और स्वाद बिल्कुल संतुलित रहे।

यह रेसिपी बिना प्याज़-लहसुन के भी बनाई जा सकती है और रोटी, बाजरे की रोटी या पराठे के साथ बहुत ही स्वादिष्ट लगती है।
अगर आप पारंपरिक राजस्थानी खाना पसंद करते हैं, तो यह रेसिपी जरूर ट्राई करें।

वीडियो पसंद आए तो लाइक करें, शेयर करें और चैनल को सब्सक्राइब करना न भूलें|

�جلیقه پرواز چه فایده ای برای پرنده دارد؟
استفاده از جلیقه پرواز پرنده باعث می شود تا پرنده شما با کاوش در فضای باز و انجام رفتارهای طبیعی مانند پرواز، نشستن و به دنبال غذا گشتن از مهارت های غریزی خود لذت برده و در نهایت باعث سلامت و شادابی پرنده شود.�

�مراحل پوشیدن جلیقه طوطی

1• پرنده را در دست بگیرید، بدنش را لمس کنید و بالهایش را باز کنید تا مطمئن شوید که پرنده به شما اعتماد دارد و از شما نمی ترسد.
پرنده را با جلیقه پرواز لمس کنید تا به آن کمی عادت کند.

2• حال باید به پرنده یاد بدهید که سرش را از حلقه سر جلیقه رد کند. این کار را می توانید با چوب آموزش که پرنده به آن اعتماد دارد انجام دهید. پرنده باید حس کند داوطلبانه در حال پوشیدن جلیقه است. بارها و بارها سر پرنده را وارد حلقه کنید و آن را به آرامی خارج کنید، با این کار پرنده یاد می گیرد که قرار نیست سرش در حلقه گیر کند و اعتمادش به حلقه جلب می شود.

3•بعد از حدود یک هفته آموزش بخش حلقه سر، می توان کل جلیقه را بست. برای این کار بخش بال های جلیقه را هم ببندید. در طول این کار هم بهتر است به پرنده خوراکی بدهید تا سرگرم شود.

�بستن جلیقه: حلقه وسط جلیقه را دور سر پرنده قرار دهید. بال‌های پرنده را بالا ببرید و دو حلقه کناری را زیر هر بال قرار دهید. مطمئن شوید که جلیقه محکم بسته شده است اما به پرنده فشار وارد نمی‌کند�

Okay my friend’s mum makes the best pineapple pachadi and I think Iam obsessed . 

You need canned or fresh pineapples that you chop into small pieces -200 g 
Crushed mustard seeds - 1 tbs 
Crushed fennel - 1 tbs 
Curry leaves - plenty 
Turmeric powder - 1/4 tbs 
Geek chillies 
Salt 
Yogurt - 1 cup 

Method

Cook the pineapple down with some water, turmeric powder and salt 
When it softens add the curry leaves and green chillies 
Then add your powders 
Cook it for few minutes until all the flavours combine 

You add your yogurt ones you are done and need to switch the gas off 

Now temper your oil with curry leaves , mustard seeds , dried red chillies . 
You are done !!

Legendary Food Points of Lucknow-
Episode 7🎥

This food spot is definitely a Hidden Gem in the narrow lanes of Sarrafa Market of Chowk’s Gol Darwaza. 
Jagdish Prasad Gupta ji travelled from Lakhimpur Kheri to Lucknow with his parents at the age of 6. That’s how Jagdish Mishthan Bhandar started at Kaliyan Tola.

They started with Khaste initially, which then became popular within Sarrafa Market. Now, after 7-8 decades they have added 12 varieties of puris to it.

They specialise in puris and khaste, a variety of pakodas, sweets and namkeen are now available too.

Sundays here are special as on Sunday you get to explore full varieties of each category here.

Find out the full story in this Reel🎞️

“Did you know Bengal’s greatest manuscripts aren’t in Bengal anymore… but in Tibet?”

After the decline of Buddhism with the rise of Hinduism and Islam, almost everything was lost. But centuries earlier, Pala monks had already carried illuminated palm-leaf manuscripts across the Himalayas to Tibet & Nepal. 
In the dry mountain air, these treasures were preserved safe in monasteries while Bengal’s humid climate destroyed nearly all local copies. Today, when we see Pala-style art in Tibetan texts, we’re looking at Bengal’s lost golden age. Bengal’s wisdom didn’t vanish; it climbed the mountains and survived.

The manuscript is called The ashtasahashrika prajnaparamita (The Perfection of Wisdom in Eight Thousand Lines)

Amils wouldn’t let their daughters marry Bhaibandhs. 

They’d say: “Unpolished. No refinement. All they know is profit.” Bhaibandhs would mock Amils at social gatherings: “Educated fools. Can’t earn a rupee to save their lives.” The insults had nicknames Amils were called scholars without substance, Bhaibandhs were called money without manners.

Even their neighborhoods were segregated. In pre-Partition Sindh, Amils lived in administrative quarters near courts and schools. Bhaibandhs lived near markets and trading hubs. The physical distance mirrored the social distance.

Before 1947, the Indus Valley (Punjab + Sindh) wasn’t two separate regions. 

It was ONE economic zone. And Multan was the city that connected it all. Khatri and Arora families moved back and forth between Lahore (Punjab) and Hyderabad (Sindh) for trade and administration. 

They kept their surnames wherever they went. That’s why today, you’ll find Khannas in both Punjab and Sindh, Chawlas in both communities, and Mehrotras who identify as either Punjabi or Sindhi.

Tag a Khanna, Chawla, or Mehrotra

❤️ Matar Ghugni (One-Pot, Comfort Food)
A simple, one-pot fresh matar ghugni made in a pressure cooker — hearty, comforting, and deeply satisfying.
🥣 Ingredients
Fresh green peas (matar) – 2 cups
Onion – 2 cups, chopped (or more, if you like it onion-forward)
Tomato – 1 medium, chopped
Potatoes (aloo) – 3–4 small, whole (or as many as you like)
Mustard oil – 2 tbsp
Ginger – 1 tbsp, grated or finely chopped (based on preference)
Garlic – 5–6 cloves, whole or chopped (based on preference)
Turmeric (haldi) – ½ tsp
Coriander powder (dhaniya powder) – 1½ tsp
Cumin powder (jeera powder) – 1 tsp
Asafoetida (hing) – a pinch
Kitchen King masala – 1 tsp
Garam masala – ½ tsp
Cinnamon – 1 small piece
Cloves – 2–3
Black cardamom – 1
Salt – to taste
Fresh coriander (dhaniya) – a handful, chopped
Water – about 1 cup
🍲 Method
📌 In a pressure cooker, add fresh matar, 2 cups chopped onion, tomato and whole potatoes
📌 Add mustard oil, ginger, garlic, haldi, dhaniya powder, jeera powder, hing, kitchen king masala, garam masala and salt
📌 Add whole spices — cinnamon, cloves and black cardamom
📌 Add fresh dhaniya and mix everything really well so the oil and masalas coat the vegetables
📌 Add about 1 cup water
(fresh matar and vegetables will release water as they cook)
📌 Close the lid and pressure cook for 3–4 whistles
2 whistles on medium flame
2 whistles on high flame
📌 Switch off the flame and let the pressure release naturally
📌 Open the cooker, gently mix and adjust salt if needed
📝 Side Note (Optional)
📌 If you don’t like the strong aroma of mustard oil, you can heat it separately till it lightly smokes, let it cool slightly, and then add it to the cooker while mixing
🍽️ To Serve
📌 Serve hot with chura, roti or bread
📌 Break the potatoes slightly while eating — that’s the best part
📌 Comfort food, no rules ❤️

❤️ Badam Diya Mula Saag
A simple, nutty radish-leaf saag cooked with peanuts and mustard oil.
🥬 Ingredients
📌 Potato – 1 medium, chopped
📌 Brinjal – 1 small, chopped
📌 Radish – 1 medium, chopped
📌 Radish leaves – 2 cups, chopped
📌 (Optional) Pumpkin – a small handful
📌 Roasted peanuts – ¼ cup
📌 Garlic – 4–5 cloves
📌 Dried red chilli – 1–2
📌 Green chilli – 1
📌 Mustard oil – 1½ tbsp
📌 Mustard seeds – 1 tsp
📌 Tomato – 1 medium, chopped
📌 Salt – to taste
👩‍🍳 Method
📌 Cook potato, brinjal, radish, radish leaves (and pumpkin, if using) together in a pot with minimal water until soft
📌 Grind peanuts, garlic, dried red chilli, and green chilli into a paste, adding water only if needed
📌 Heat mustard oil in a kadai until lightly smoking
📌 Add mustard seeds and let them splutter
📌 Add dried red chilli
📌 Add the peanut paste and cook for 3–4 minutes on low flame, stirring well
📌 Add salt and tomatoes and cook until tomatoes soften
📌 Add the cooked vegetables and mix well
📌 Adjust water according to desired consistency
📌 Simmer for 2–3 minutes and switch off
🔢 Approximate Macros (per serving)
📌 Calories: ~230 kcal
📌 Protein: ~7 g
📌 Carbs: ~23 g
📌 Fat: ~14 g

Shio Koji “Bacon” (Video-Style Easy Koji Bacon)

-What this is?

A bacon-style pork belly made by curing with shio koji (salted rice koji) and then baking at low temperature. Shio koji tenderizes the meat and boosts umami.
Note: This is not the same as “pink cured” smoked bacon made with curing salts.

-Ingredients (makes 500g–1kg)
Pork belly block: 500g to 1kg
Shio koji: ~10% of the meat weight
500g pork → 50g shio koji
1kg pork → 100g shio koji
Coarsely ground black pepper: to taste (generous, like the video)
Black tea leaves (dry): a handful (about 2–3 tbsp) for aroma
(Optional) Smoking chips / smoker / smoke gun for extra smoke

-Method
1) Quick cure (about 3 minutes)
Pat the pork belly dry.
Rub shio koji over all surfaces (or add both to a bag and massage well).
Press out air and seal tightly.
2) Refrigerate for 3 days
Cure in the fridge for 3 days.
Flip the bag once a day for even curing.
3) Wipe off the shio koji (do not rinse)
Remove pork from the bag.
Thoroughly wipe off the shio koji with paper towels.
Wiping (instead of rinsing) keeps umami on the surface and helps it dry.
4) Season with black pepper
Place pork on a rack and coat all sides with black pepper (including the fat side).
5) Bake with tea aroma: 120°C for 70 minutes
Scatter black tea leaves on a baking tray.
Place a rack over the tray (so the pork does not touch the tea leaves).
Put pork on the rack.
Bake at 120°C (250°F) for 70 minutes.
Ventilate well—tea leaves can create aroma and light smoke.
6) Optional: extra smoke
The video adds extra smoking after baking.
If you have a smoker/smoke gun, add a short smoke session (5–15 minutes) for aroma.
7) Cool, then slice
Cool to room temp, then chill in the fridge for clean slicing.
Slice thin or thick as you like.

When we installed this loft net I had an idea that my cats might enjoy it but I never had any idea just how much Mog would love it! Mog has endless fun watching the birds from the window and swooshing her tail at them (she can’t get them I promise!) She also loves to take naps here and weirdly I think she prefers napping on the net without a blanket underneath!

This is why your partner can be the best ADHD resource:

1. External structure without shame
They help anchor routines (like brushing teeth together) without feeling like a reminder alarm yelling at you.

2. Body-doubling actually works
Doing mundane tasks side-by-side reduces avoidance and boosts follow-through—your nervous system stays regulated.

3. They notice patterns you can’t see
ADHD blinds us to cycles. A partner often sees when you’re overstimulated, burnt out, or drifting before you do.

4. Emotional regulation by proximity
Co-regulation matters. Simply being near someone safe can calm impulsivity, anxiety, and overwhelm.

5. Consistency beats motivation
ADHD doesn’t need more willpower… it needs routine. A partner helps turn habits into shared rituals.

#adhd #adhdtips #adhdtherapy #mentalhealth #adhdtherapist

From people with low blood pressure and IBS to those on blood thinners or with kidney issues, chia can sometimes do more harm than good. 

Always consume mindfully, 1–2 tablespoons a day, with plenty of water.

🗝Exclusive for pro chefs! Meet Salted Bafun Uni in an ultra-thin pressed sheet. 

What is Salted Bafun Uni ("Shio Uni” in Japanese)?

It's one of Japan’s “Three Great Delicacies” alongside bottarga. Creamy, briny, and umami-rich, aged in Fukui by artisans who blend sea urchins from different coasts to create its signature balance.

About 200 years ago, the artisans of Fukui created Shio Uni for the nobility to indulge in. They had to make the sea urchin last during the long war period, so they salted it for preservation. 

This pressed sheet is pliable enough to wrap, drape, fold, or slice into precise ribbons. On the palate, it melts instantly unfolding layers of briny sweetness, ocean aroma, and concentrated umami. Perfect for topping warm grains, pairing with proteins, or adding an umami-rich accent to crudo, hors d’oeuvres, or tasting-menu bites. 

📌Pro chefs, save this post for your next menu build.

TRANSCENDENT MEMORIES 2.0 

Memory does not remain still - it grows, accumulates, and transforms.

Transcendent Memories 2.0 is a continuation of the journey that began earlier from his mother’s garden where the everyday harvest he grew up with became vessels of storytelling - through touch, texture, and personal ritual.

That that narrative unfolds further. What began as quiet brass casts - later etched with delicate motifs in Transcendent Memories - continues and now finds fresh momentum. The surfaces are animated with dotted patterns that shift memory into a tactile realm and stretch the boundaries of his earlier work. The familiar shapes return, carrying the past, yet they open to unexpected possibilities - as if memory itself is learning to speak a new language.

His work Jodhpur Art Week also join this presentation, connecting his personal landscape to a wider world and expanding the arc of his narrative.

Transcendent Memories 2.0 is not a sequel - it is a rooted evolution.
A return that moves forward.
A memory that keeps growing.

Here’s what actually changed everything for us:
	
1. We shifted the mindset from “your mood is my responsibility” to “your emotions are yours, and I can stay grounded anyway.”
	
2. We trust each other with the full weight of our honesty. Not watered down. Not padded to protect feelings. The kind of truth that would make our parents choke, shared because we know our relationship can hold it.
	
3. We let anger, irritation, and frustration exist without turning each other into the problem. We get annoyed. We get irritated. And we don’t take it personally.

We regulate ourselves instead of managing each other.

Before, one of us would get frustrated and the other would instantly shrink, feel responsible, and try to fix it. Now we allow emotions to move through without assigning blame.

The real hack isn’t emotional control.
It’s remembering we are not responsible for each other’s emotions while still making room for each other to be fully human.

That’s when the obsession hit.

𝘊𝘢𝘣𝘢𝘪 𝘑𝘢𝘸𝘢 / Javanese Long Pepper 🌶️

Cabai jawa is one of those spices many Indonesians recognize by name, but rarely by use. Today it’s considered scarce even in local markets, often sold dried and relegated to jamu and medicinal drinks, while its fresh form remains largely unfamiliar in everyday cooking.

Long pepper itself is anything but new. Archaeological evidence shows that Piper longum was already being traded thousands of years ago, with remains and residues found in ancient Mesopotamian contexts along river-based trade routes — carried from South and Southeast Asia as both spice and medicine. Its presence there speaks less to novelty, and more to how deeply embedded this spice once was in early human diets.

This curiosity took me to Kulon Progo, where I met Bu Sukma, whose family has been growing cabai jawa. Walking through their farm and listening to their stories reminded me that many culinary records in Indonesia were passed down orally, long before they were written down. One dish they still cook with fresh cabai jawa is pepes.

Pepes can be many things — most commonly fish, an ancient and ancestral protein. But in this video, I’m making pepes jantung pisang (banana blossom), a preparation that sits between pepes and botok, because I use grated coconut. One small but important detail: banana blossoms should be sliced directly into boiling salted water to prevent oxidation and bitterness.

⸻

Pepes Jantung Pisang Cabai Jawa:

	•	500 g jantung pisang (banana blossom), thinly sliced
	•	1 tbsp sea salt
	•	80 g grated coconut (slightly coarse)
	•	3–4 fresh cabai jawa
	•	4 shallots
	•	3 cloves garlic
	•	3 cm galangal
	•	2 cm turmeric
	•	2 cm ginger
	•	3 candlenuts
	•	a handful of lemon basil
	•	4 bay leaves
	•	6 lime leaves
	•	Banana leaves, for wrapping

I owe a credit to @mgdalenaf for introducing me to Bu Sukma, so here’s an appreciation post for her to have uplifted this endangered knowledge 🤍

料理が終わった後も、パラレロシンクの使いやすさは続きます。

広いシンクに、鍋やフライパンをまとめて置いても余裕。 二人並んで、洗う・すすぐ作業を同時に進められるから、後片付けのテンポが変わります。
さらに、両側からアクセスできるパラレロシンクなら、向かい合って ・洗う人 ・拭いて片付ける人 と役割分担もスムーズ。

「誰か一人がやる家事」から、「自然と分担できる後片付け」へ。

調理だけでなく、後片付けまで一歩も動かずに完結する。 それが、パラレロシンクのあるゼロ動線キッチンです。

How clever is this pull-out desktop by @lozi_designs?
A beautifully considered piece of multifunctional joinery, it glides out to create a generous workspace when you need it, then tucks seamlessly back into the cabinetry when you don’t. The perfect balance of flexibility, functionality and clean design.

We love solutions like this for small spaces, home offices, kids’ study zones, or even commercial workspaces where adaptability is key. It’s a reminder that smart joinery can completely transform the way a room works — without compromising aesthetics.

A minimal, streamlined, space-saving design moment that proves great working-from-home setups don’t need a whole room… just thoughtful design. ✨

This feels like a proper “Vasanthi Recommends” list — no gimmicks, only classic Bangalore food that has stood the test of time.
Idli at Raghavendra Stores near Malleshwaram railway station — the kind of place where stepping off a train and eating on the platform feels like tradition.�Filter coffee and masala dose at Konark on Residency Road, one of those last standing Bangalore institutions that still tastes exactly how it should.�That unbeatable chutney and bonda soup at Navya Samrat, Infantry Road — proper Bangalore style, with coconut pieces, not grated, just the way locals swear by.�And finally, holige at Nisarga, best enjoyed only at the right time — around 4 PM, not before, not after.
This isn’t just a food list. It’s a map of memories, neighbourhoods, and flavours that make Bengaluru what it is. If you love Bangalore, you already know why these places matter.

[Jimmy Jimmy, Jimmy Jimmy The Show, Bangalore, Vasanthi Hariprakash, Bangalore Stories, Bangalore Podcast, Namma Bengaluru, Bangalore history, Bangalore Trivia, old bangalore, bangalore diaries, Bangalore nostalgia, Bangalore culture, Kannada, multilingual Bangalore, podcast India, interview podcast Bangalore, Malleshwaram, Radio City, private radio, fm radio] 

Is it Malleshwara or Malleshwaram?
That question becomes the starting point for a much larger conversation about Bengaluru, belonging, language, memory, and the quiet emotions that shape a city. In this full episode, Vasanthi Hariprakash reflects on how sitto — not anger, but a gentle unease — has been building in the city over the last decade, turning everyday words into markers of identity 
From arriving in Bangalore in 1990 and thinking it felt like a hill station, to studying at MES College, finding her voice through journalism, Aakashvani, and Radio City, this episode traces a deeply personal journey that mirrors the city itself. It moves fluidly across languages — Kannada, Tamil, Hindi, Urdu, Sanskrit, Dakni — showing how language is not just communication, but connection. A single word spoken right can melt barriers, spark recognition, and make a city feel like home.
The conversation travels through sarees and memory, community and in

China’s youth have found a new kind of pet, and it doesn’t need food, walks, or even a cage. It just needs a bottle of tea and a few days.

On social media, young people are intentionally growing what they call “tea fungi” inside bottled tea drinks. Leave the bottle open, keep it warm, and a fuzzy, translucent blob slowly forms. Some grow to the size of a ping-pong ball. Others get names. None of them are meant to be consumed.

HR ne PIP (Performance Improvement Plan) diya hai? Galti se bhi RESIGN mat karna! 🛑😤

Yeh Corporate World ka sabse purana trick hai. Company aapko seedha fire nahi karna chahti kyunki unhe ‘Severance Pay’ dena padega. Isliye wo PIP ka drama karte hain taaki aap darr ke maare khud quit kar do.

Here is the Law: Putting you on a PIP is fine, but forcing you to resign is Constructive Dismissal (Illegal). ✅ Strategy: PIP sign karo par usme likho: “I disagree with this assessment. My performance is up to the mark.” Make a paper trail! 📝

Agar company zabardasti nikaale, toh yeh Wrongful Termination hai. Hum court mein challenge kar sakte hain. Darna band karo!

👇 Need a Lawyer’s Strategy? Agar aap PIP par hain aur career bachana chahte hain, toh risk mat lo. Click the Link in Bio to Book a 15-Min Strategy Call with me, or you BBC an get your complaint registered in the Grievance Cell. 📞⚖️

Never thought a wild forest flower would change the way I sweeten my coffee.

After a number of trips, countless rounds of tastings, and a whole lot of learning from the Gond community in the forests of central India - this age-old mahua nectar recipe finally made its way into a bottle that I could share with you.

Made using traditional overnight techniques, just like it’s been for generations - this nectar is rich in iron, good for haemoglobin, and tastes like caramel sunshine.

Vegan, wild, and deeply rooted in the forest.

Over the weekend I had the honour of cooking a private eight course dinner for a good mate’s husband’s 40th. The brief was very him, big Malaysian flavours, a proper steak with gravy, not huge on seafood, and of course… fried chicken absolutely had to be on the menu.

This was the bread course, the only dish I managed to get on camera. And in true me fashion, I didn’t hit record for the reveal, and didn’t have a spare loaf to redo it 😂 That’s what happens when you’re riding solo, juggling eight courses, and trying to make everything just right, whilst working a full time job. The night progressively became more of a challenge as I was getting fed Hennessy shots every 15 mins. I’m not really a drinker but hey, we’re talking about a bespoke experience here. 

If you’ve been following along, you’ll know I’ve got a real soft spot for cooking things inside sourdough. What looks like a simple little roll was honestly packed punches of flavour.

A 24-hour fermented dough wrapped around beef short rib rendang that had been slow-cooked for 12 hours until it melted, studded with smoked bone marrow, then hand wrapped one by one before baking them until perfectly crisp. Finished with a brush of my fried onion infused beef tallow. I  baked them again at their place to get that extra golden crust. Pretty stoked with how they turned out, check out those little ears! The loaf was served with a fermented chilli butter, with a good smack of lemongrass from Vietnamese sate, then spiked with finely chopped lime leaf and coriander root. Fresh, spicy and sour to cut through the richness of the marrow. 

Nights like this make the rare private dinners worth it. It took me weeks to plan, big flavours, custom menus, and cooking that feels personal. Really grateful for it.

Big shoutout to @jundakhoo_hojiak for hooking us up with some Malaysian ingredients. Made my life a whole lot easier 🙌

Full menu at the end of the vid!

I’ll definitely have to make this one again for a reveal video!

There are dozens of persian stews, or khoresht, out there but to me fesenjan is the most unique. It’s a pomegranate and walnut stew, sweet and rich but balanced by the sourness of the pomegranate. Here we’re going with duck for that elevated feel, which simmers in that goodness until pull apart tender. 

Ingredients 1 large onion, finely diced
3 medjool dates
5 oz walnuts
1 pinch saffron
1/4 cup water (for blooming saffron)
1/2 teaspoon turmeric
1/2 teaspoon black pepper
1/3 cup pomegranate molasses
2 duck leg quarters (or chicken leg quarters)
1 cinnamon stick
2 cups water
Salt, to taste
Pomegranate seeds, to garnish
Optional sugar, to taste

Micro-concrete is a seamless, joint-free finish that gives any space a clean, modern look. It’s a 2 mm cement-based coating applied directly over old tiles, wood, or even walls—no demolition needed. Highly versatile, it works on floors, walls, furniture, and bathrooms, and comes in multiple colours, textures, and finishes. It’s durable and low-maintenance, but requires skilled application and typically costs more than tiles. Designers love it for its sleek, industrial-chick aesthetic that suits both minimal and contemporary spaces.

I have been making this RAGI UPPU URUNDAI 😍 from a last few years as a healthy snack or breakfast in my home, and everyone loves it. I sometimes even pack it for my husband’s Lunch box with some hot sambar and chutney.

I personally love it with tomato onion chutney, but my family loves to eat it with coconut chutney. 🌱

I have used a mix of rice and ragi in this, but if you are looking for diabetic friendly recipes, you can just take it with ragi flour too. 😍

For the Diabetic friendly version, you can leave a comment and I will message you the detailed recipe. 

RECIPE - 
Add 1 cup soaked rice, 1/4 cup fresh coconut, salt to taste and 1/2 cup ragi flour in a blender and blend till it’s slightly coarse. Once done, keep aside. 
Heat 1 tbsp oil, add 1 tsp mustard seeds, 1 tsp urad dal, 1 tsp chana dal, 3-4 cashews (broken), 2 dry red chillies (crushed), 2 sprig curry leaves and 1/4 cup fresh coconut (grated).
Add in the prepared ragi rice batter and cook on low heat until it becomes thick. Now make it small balls and steam for 8-10 minutes. Serve with your favourite chutney.

hyderabadi marag 

recipe:

1. Take 10 cashews and 10 almonds in a cup and add 1 cup milk. let them soak for 30 minutes. after 30 minutes, grind this into a paste and keep aside. 
2. Add 5tbsp ghee in a pot, add 2 tbsp ginger garlic paste, add 2 small cinnamon sticks, 3 cardamom pods, 1tsp whole black peppercorns, 1 tsp kabab chini, 3 cloves, 1 tsp shahzeera. Saute for 2-3 minutes. 
3. Add 4 chopped green chillies and saute for few minutes. 
4. Add half a sliced onion and cook for 3 minutes. Then add 650 gm medium cut lamb/mutton (bone-in). 
5. Add 1/4th tsp turmeric powder, salt to taste, 1/2 cups plain yoghurt. Add 3 cups of water. To save time, i used a pressure cooker but traditionally it’s slow cooked. if you’re using a pressure cooker, let it whistle 3-4 times and leave it on simmer for 15 minutes. Check to see if the meat is tender. If not cook for some more time 
6. Add the cashew and almond paste. Add 3 cups water and let it come to a boil. 
7. Add a pinch of saffron, 1.5 tsp black pepper powder, 1 tsp garam masala powder, 1/4th cup chopped mint and 1/4th cup chopped cilantro, 1/2 cup heavy cream. 
8. Cook for 15 minutes on a medium to low flame and it’s ready! 
9. Serve with soft naan.

PORK PRAWN & PEAS 🫛 

Made with @leekumkeeaustralia gluten free condiments and enjoyed with multiple bowls of rice 🍚🍚🍚

Serves 1-2
200g pork mince
100g prawn meat minced
3 scallions sliced
3 garlic cloves minced
1 tbsp minced ginger
2 tbsp Lee Kum Kee Gluten Free Panda Brand Oyster Sauce
2 tbsp Lee Kum Kee Gluten Free Soy Sauce
1 tbsp sugar

250g snow peas
2 tbsp cornstarch

1 tsp dried anchovy minced/ 1 Italian anchovy
1 tsp minced ginger
1 tsp minced chilli
3 tbsp neutral oil
1 tbsp soy sauce
1/2 tbsp oyster sauce
1 tbsp white vinegar

Combine filling ingredients until a uniform mass and sticky. Top and tail snow peas then use a skewer to open. Dust worn cornstarch then fill. Fry until golden brown. Serve with anchovy and ginger dressing 🥢

Balcony Makeover bhi kar diya, ab toh my sister should start paying me now 😆

Aur aap log ki bhi kaafi requests thi DM me ki Balcony Makeover karo, so ho gaya ! Ab kaisa lag raha hai, jaldi jaldi comments mein batao 👇🏻

Let’s start with the LIGHTING 💡
Kyuki jab tak outdoor spaces mein achi light na ho toh maza nahi aata hai and @bajajelectricals_ltd Velaris Wall Mount lights were the best choice. Sleek design hai, 2 colour variants me available hai and not too loud, not too dim just the perfect warm inviting ambient lights. Definitely check out karna saare 👍🏻

➡️ MOSAIC WALL
I love Goa for its mosaic art and had to bring in a part of it into the balcony. So ply ke cutouts liye, unpe broken tile pieces (available on Amazon) lagaya using nail free glue and filled the gaps with white grout. Then assembled all onto the wall one by one. 

➡️ FURNITURE REVAMP
Trust me the easiest part is painting your old furniture. Bas sand karna hai, then add 1-2 layers of wood primer and colour of your choice (I used chalk paints here). Upholstery change karlo if possible and don’t forget a varnish layer on coloured part. 

➡️ VERTICAL PLANTS
Use an adjustable height decorating rod over your railing and hang plant baskets there. Koi dead space ho then cover it with mesh panels and add vertical plants on them. And 2 hanging tables with plant baskets in front makes a great addition as well. 

➡️ FLOORING 
Acacia wooden deck tiles are meant for outdoor use but they can be expensive. So you can divide your flooring in 60:40 ratio and have good quality artificial turf grass along with deck tiles and white stones. 

➡️ DECOR
Add statement decor pieces with few functional utilities, always in a set of 3 or 5 (odd nos only). 

That’s it, my SISTER’s BALCONY was readdyyyy 🪴

If I told you there’s a single spray that keeps cockroaches, mosquitoes, flies, and even bed bugs away for an entire month, what would your reaction be? Temprid SC is one of the strongest household insect control solutions from Envu (formerly Bayer), and its results are genuinely impressive.

The method is extremely simple: take one litre of water, add 4 ml of Temprid SC, mix it well, and spray it around the corners, gaps, drain areas, door frames, and all the spots where insects enter your home. After spraying, let it dry for two hours. Once dry, the surface is safe for kids and pets.

A single application gives around 30 days of protection. No cockroaches, no mosquitoes, no flies, and no bed bugs. It’s also odourless, which makes it perfect for bedrooms, kitchens, and closed spaces where chemical smells usually become a problem.

Envu is known globally for its pest control products, and Temprid SC is one of their most reliable options for long-term home protection.

Kashmiri harissa & bread 😋

Cook with comali 6 recipe recreation - Recipe 28

Video credits - Cook with comali 6 vijay tv available in Jio hot star 

Recipe inspired from @mir.umair.official 

A quick recipe 
Mutton harissa 
🥩To a pressure cooker add 1/2 mutton boneless and 5 cups water and pressure cook 6-7 whistle.
🥩Use fork and shred the meat and keep the stock.
🥩Now gradually add the stock (2 cups) and keep stirring it and mash it.
🥩To a bowl add 2 cup stock &1/2 cup rice flour and mix without any lumps.
🥩Now add the rice flour mix gradually and keep mixing it.
🥩Now add 1/2 cup fried onion, 1/4 cup fried onion oil and keep mixing in low flame.
🥩Simmer until thick, creamy, and the texture is almost like haleem.

Kashmiri bread 
🫓To a bowl add 1 tbsp yeast, 1 tbsp sugar,1/4 cup warm water and mix well.
🫓Rest for 10 min.
🫓To a bowl add 1&1/2 cup Maida, salt, ghee 1 tbsp, milk powder 2 tbsp, baking powder 1 tsp and mix well.
🫓Add yeast mix and water gradually make a soft dough.
🫓Rest for 2 hours and flatten the dough.
🫓Use your fingertips or back of a spoon to press traditional circle patterns or dimples on top
🫓Brush milk over the outer surface and bake it.
🫓Preheat the oven for 10 min 200°C for 10 min.
🫓Bake for 10 min and heat the upper rod to get a golden crust.
🫓Brush ghee/butter for extra flavour.

Looking for the perfect self closing door hinges India for your home or commercial space? 🚪✨
In today’s modern interiors, automatic door closer hinges are becoming essential for smooth, silent, hands-free convenience. Whether you’re exploring different auto close hinges types or searching for the most durable door closer hinges heavy duty, choosing the right hardware can make a huge difference in your door’s performance and safety.

Many homeowners and contractors prefer heavy duty spring hinge door closers because they offer long-lasting strength, reliable closing action, and easy installation. If you need a premium option, the best automatic door closer hinges combine durability with sleek design—perfect for offices, shops, and luxury homes.

To get the best performance, proper door closer adjustment is crucial. Adjusting speed, tension, and alignment helps ensure smooth closing, prevents door slamming, and increases the lifespan of the hinges. Whether you’re a professional installer or upgrading your home, understanding how to correctly tune these settings is key to flawless results.

Before buying, always compare door closer price based on build quality, brand reputation, and warranty. Heavy-duty hinges may cost more, but they offer superior reliability and safety. Also, knowing the essential door closer parts—like the spring mechanism, hinge plates, tension screws, and hydraulic control modules—helps you choose the right model for long-term use.

From residential interiors to commercial entrances, the right automatic hinge system brings convenience, security, and style to your doors. If you’re planning an upgrade, now is the best time to switch to high-quality self-closing hinges and experience the difference!

📍Mátra Borozó (Budapest, Corvin tér 1, 1011) 

🇭🇺🍷It’s a small, simple wine bar — rare and unique these days — and it feels like stepping back into the old times. It’s very hard to find places like this in Budapest, which makes it even more special.

I suggest going in the afternoon when it’s less busy (it gets extremely crowded in the evening). Don’t set your expectations too high — take it as an adventure. If you want to see the real Hungary, this is part of it! 🇭🇺

Back when I lived in Korea in my 20s, a dish called pa-dak (fried chicken with shredded green onion and sweet-soy sauce) was everywhere. I used to get it all the time in my college days — crispy, saucy, salty-sweet perfection😋

Recently, a celeb reintroduced this non-deep-fried version of pa-dak-style pan-fried chicken with green onion (파닭전) — and I finally tried making it, with a few tweaks of my own. It turned out SO good — and honestly, the flavors brought me right back to my go-to late-night spots in Seoul 🥹💛

I used the CHEFWIN 316 stainless steel pan and was seriously impressed by how evenly it cooked everything — crispy edges, juicy chicken, no sticking. The rivet-free handle also makes cleanup so much easier.

The 316 stainless steel is also way more resistant to rust and discoloration than typical pans — definitely a piece I’ll keep reaching for.

🥘Padakjeon (Korean-style Pan-fried Chicken with Green Onion)

Ingredients:

1lb chicken thigh
2 tbsp cooking wine (matsul)
Salt and pepper
5–6 green chili peppers, chopped (optional)
2–3 red chili peppers, chopped (optional)
4–5 garlic cloves, thinly sliced + 1 tbsp minced garlic (for sauce)
1 tbsp fish sauce
½ cup potato starch
About 4 tbsp water
3–4 green onions, thinly sliced
Oil for frying

Sauce:

2 tbsp soy sauce
2 tbsp cooking wine (matsul)
2 tbsp mulyeot (Korean corn syrup)
1 tbsp minced garlic
1 tbsp sugar

Instructions:

1. Marinate chicken:
 Chop chicken, and marinate with 2 tbsp cooking wine, salt, and pepper for 30 minutes.
2. Make batter:
 Mix chicken with chili peppers, sliced garlic, fish sauce, potato starch, and water.
3. Prep green onions:
 Soak sliced green onions in ice water for 10 minutes, then drain.
4. Prepare sauce:
 Combine soy sauce, cooking wine, mulyeot, minced garlic, and sugar.
5. Cook :
 Heat oil in pan over medium heat. Spoon batter, spread slightly, and fry 4–5 minutes per side until golden and cooked.
6. Serve:
 Layer the chicken, top with green onions, and drizzle sauce over.

—
This product was provided for review, but all opinions are my own.

Did you know you can turn chamomile tea into a crisp and relaxing champagne?

🥂 Chamomile Champagne

INGREDIENTS
* 5 cups water
* 3 tbsp chamomile buds
* Peel from 1 lemon (avoid white pith)
* 8 thin slices fresh ginger
* 5 cardamom pods, lightly crushed
* 1 star anise
* 10 black peppercorns
* 2 tsp agave syrup
* Small pinch of sea salt
* ¼ tsp citric acid

PREPARATION
1️⃣ Simmer water with ginger, cardamom, star anise, peppercorns, and lemon peel for 10 minutes
2️⃣ Remove from heat, add chamomile, and steep with lid on for 5 minutes
3️⃣  While still warm, stir in agave, salt, and citric acid until dissolved
4️⃣ Fine strain through a coffee filter so no particles remain
5️⃣ Chill in fridge until fully cold before fizzing
6️⃣ Using a carbonated drink maker, fizz in short bursts, then slow-vent in tiny releases—pause if foam rises
7️⃣ Chill in sealed carbonation bottle until ready to serve

STORAGE
* Best stored in sealed carbonation bottle for up to 12 hours
* For a festive presentation, transfer to a flip top glass bottle before serving

TIPS
* IMPORTANT: Most soda siphons and SodaStreams are designed for water-only and will foam out or void warranties when used with tea or infused liquids.
* I used the @drinkmate_usa OmniFizz, which is designed to carbonate tea, juice, cocktails using a slow-release valve that prevents blowouts.
* No carbonation machine? Skip steps 6-7. Brew more concentrated using half the water, chill, then mix ½ tea concentrate : ½ cold club soda in-glass when serving.

MODIFICATIONS
* Want it sweeter? This version is intentionally dry/brut. If you normally prefer sweeter sparkling wines, simply increase agave or mix in white grape juice before carbonating.
* No citric acid? You can use juice from ½ lemon, but you’ll lose some of the crispness and clarity.

Yuzu Kosho. 

Equal parts green chilli and yuzu zest combined with 15% of that total weight in salt. Young/green yuzu has a higher amount of essential oils in the skin so is better for this application. Avoid iodised salt. 

Grind into a paste, store in an airtight container and leave out at room temperature for a week (lacto-fermentation). Release the gas every day or 2 so it doesn’t explode. After a week of fermentation, move to the fridge.

Butterfly Shrimp Chips🍤 @shrimphappyprotein One bite and you’re hooked. It melts in your mouth, and fuels your body with protein, omega-3s, and essential vitamins. Done.✔️ #ShrimpMadeEasy #HealthyAndDelicious #ad
 
Full Recipe below👇

12 shrimp (peeled and deveined)
3 large  rice paper quartered
Pinch of salt 
Pinch of pepper
For frying -3 cups neutral oil

For dipping sauce
1/4 ranch dressing
1 tbsp chili garlic crisp

Instructions
Prepare the rice paper by cutting it into quarters using kitchen shears

Assemble shrimp chips:
    •    �Lay a piece of (dried) rice paper flat. Position a flattened shrimp in the center and the tail on the end. Pound and flatten the shrimp on top of the rice paper until very thin. Season with salt and pepper
    •    �Repeat with the rest of the shrimp and rice paper.
 Fry the shrimp chips:
    ⁃    In a Medium pot, heat the oil to 375°F. Drop shrimp chip carefully and cook for about 1-2 mins until shrimp turns pink. Remove from heat and let rest or wire rack or sheet tray lined with paper towels. Serve with Chili Ranch and Devour!

Not available in the market today, this rare collectible captures the nostalgic spirit of old Japan. You can also repurpose it as a unique storage or organizer piece with a touch of Japan, perfect for your home, café, or workspace.

They look like minibar shots, but they’re actually for sleep.

💤🥃 SLEEP SHOTS 

INGREDIENTS
* 2½ cups water
* 1 tbsp dried hibiscus flowers
* 2-4 tbsp dried valerian root (start with 2 tbsp, use 4 for stronger effect)
* 1–2 tbsp dried passionflower (start with 1 tbsp, use 2 for stronger effect)
* Peel from 2 oranges (just the zest, no pith)
* 3 green cardamom pods, crushed
* 1-inch fresh ginger, sliced
* 8 black peppercorns
* 1 tbsp maple syrup
* 1½ cups pomegranate OR tart cherry juice
* Juice of 1 lemon OR ½ tsp citric acid (for longer fridge storage)
* Pinch of sea salt
* 6–8 dashes aromatic bitters

PREPARATION
1️⃣ Bring water to boil, add all herbs and spices, reduce to a gentle simmer for 5 minutes.
2️⃣ Remove from heat, cover, and steep 10 min. You can sterilize bottles in another pot while you wait.
3️⃣ Strain the tea infusion through fine mesh, then stir in maple syrup, juice, salt, bitters, and either lemon OR citric acid.
4️⃣ Pour warm liquid into glass bottles, fill close to top, seal airtight, refrigerate.

STORAGE
* Keep sealed and refrigerated for up to 4–5 days if using lemon juice OR 7–10 days with citric acid.
* For longest fridge life, sterilize bottles 10 min in boiling water

HOW TO SERVE
* Pour over ice with a garnish, or top with seltzer for a spritz.

MODIFICATIONS
➡️ Avoiding valerian? Swap it for chamomile.
➡️ Tart cherry or pomegranate? They’re both rich in antioxidants, and mask the valerian flavor beautifully. Tart cherry has a touch of melatonin making, it a great choice.
➡️ Mixing with alcohol? A small splash is fine for flavor, but remember alcohol can disrupt sleep cycles and typically not advised to mix with valerian.

CONSIDERATIONS
* New to these herbs? Test a small amount first to see how your body responds before making a batch.
* Pregnant or nursing? Avoid passionflower, hibiscus, and valerian, and always check with your doctor.
* Taking sedatives or antidepressants? Skip the valerian.

#driftology #alcoholfree #bettersleep

MAKIKO - Hiroaki Onishi @hiroakionishi0024 

This shelf is entirely constructed from Echizen washi, with a mechanism that winds the paper around the rear support pillars to open and close the doors. The act of opening the doors naturally evokes a sense of tension, reminiscent of unrolling a scroll or performing the ritual of opening a fusuma door. The friction of the washi and its delicate texture enhance the tension in the moment of release, deepening its ritualistic significance through both sight and touch. To highlight the beautiful surface of the Echizen washi, the shelf's structure is crafted from primitive wood, creating a dignified presence that recalls the atmosphere of shrines and temples.

全ての面に越前和紙を用い、背面の支柱に巻き込まれる仕組みで扉が開閉するシェルフ。扉を開ける動作は、自然と緊張感を伴い、巻物を広げる瞬間や襖を開ける儀式的な所作を彷彿とさせます。和紙が擦れ合う音と繊細な触感が、その開放の瞬間に緊張を高め、視覚と触覚を通じて、儀礼的な意味を深めていきます。越前和紙の美しい面の表情を引き立たせるためプリミティブな形の木材で構成、神社や仏閣を思わせる厳かな佇まいを創り出しています。

💰 Japan 12-Day Budget Breakdown for two

This does not include flight or visa costs. 

Food: $688 NZD / ₹35,619
Accommodation: $1,089 NZD / ₹56,380
Transport: $540 NZD / ₹27,957
Activities: $600 NZD / ₹31,063
Total: $2,917 NZD / ₹1.5L

We explored Tokyo, Kyoto, Nara & Osaka, staying in a mix of hotels + traditional ryokans. Clean, comfortable, slightly outside tourist hubs, averaging ~$108 a night.

📍 Itinerary
– Tokyo: 4 days – quirky neighborhoods, iconic sights, ramen galore
– Kyoto: 4 days – temples, shrines & hidden gems
– Nara: 1 day – deer park & small-town charm (day trip)
– Osaka: 3 days – Dotonbori, street food, nightlife

🚆 Transport
Suica card + 72-hour Tokyo Metro Pass + local/bullet trains & buses = ~$540 NZD. Smooth, convenient, much better than taxis or flights.

🍜 Food
– Ramen: ¥500 (cheap) to ¥1,400 (Ichiran splurge)
– Izakayas, street food & konbini meals = cheap, fast, authentic

🏯 Activities
– Mt. Fuji day trip
– Kyoto temples & shrines
– Nara deer park
– Dotonbori street food
Most free or under $15 NZD / ₹776.

💡 Budget Tips
– Stay just outside tourist hubs
– Suica + metro pass combo
– Convenience stores for meals
– Add free attractions to routes

🏙️ Highlights
– Tokyo: quirky hidden streets
– Kyoto: peaceful mountain temples
– Osaka: food heaven
– Nara: friendly deer & charm

❤️ Our Picks
Kyoto for temples, Osaka for food, Tokyo for energy. Add Hiroshima as a day trip from Osaka if you love history.

This artwork, titled Kālī Āvāhan , is inspired by Arjuna’s invocation of Goddess Durga (Kali) to obtain the boon of victory in the battle of Mahābhārat. 

This artwork depicts Arjuna’s symbolic invocation of the universe’s omnipresent power through a spiral arrangement of 108 flowers. As he receives the potent energy (shakti), a protective circle of 108 Navavarna Mantras manifests, encapsulating him in a vortex of spiritual protection and empowerment.

In this episode, we journey through Bengaluru’s unique heritage, uncovering stories that connect the city’s past with its present.

Join Jimmy aka jimmy da jimmy and Prem Koshy as we explore the enduring legacy of Koshys, a cherished Bengaluru institution where generations have shared meals, memories, and moments of celebration. We also dive into the cultural heartbeat of the city by revisiting the Bowring Club and the beloved Top Kaapi, places that have shaped social life and community in Bangalore.

Bengaluru’s history is a tapestry of diverse influences and creative energy. We discuss how the city’s evolving identity—its architecture, neighborhoods, and cultural scenes—has influenced its culinary landscape, from iconic idlis and dosas to the beloved roasted chicken favorites that locals and visitors savor today. The episode highlights how Bangalore’s streets and landmarks, including the 13th Floor, have become symbols of democratic space, community, and connection.

Koshys played a pivotal role not only in dining but also in Bengaluru’s arts and cultural scene, where music, theatre, and shared experiences flourish. Expect unexpected anecdotes, heartfelt moments, and the quintessential Bengaluru charm as we paint a portrait of a city that continually blends tradition with modern ambition.

Cannabis increases oxygen efficiency — so you can train harder and recover faster.
Oxygen efficiency isn’t just about lungs — it’s about how well your body delivers and uses oxygen at the cellular level.
The better that system runs, the longer you can perform before fatigue sets in.

Data:
Studies in Frontiers in Pharmacology (2022) and European Journal of Applied Physiology (2021) found cannabinoids enhance vasodilation and oxygen delivery, improving circulation and recovery efficiency.
More oxygen = more endurance, faster healing.

Mechanism:
Cannabinoids activate CB1 receptors in blood vessels, stimulating nitric oxide release and improving flow to muscle tissue.
That means better energy metabolism and faster removal of waste like CO₂ and lactic acid.

Application:
Low, intentional doses of cannabis can support training adaptation:
🌿 Pre-workout THC (microdose): improves focus and oxygen uptake
🌿 Post-workout CBD: reduces inflammation and restores circulation
Pair with breathwork, hydration, and sleep for maximum results.

⁠
Diving into a work by Ksawery Komputery means entering a space where code becomes material, light becomes a visual language, and movement becomes emotion.⁠
⁠
At the intersection of digital and generative art, the studio develops interactive installations and audiovisual performances that merge design, technology, and algorithmic aesthetics. Light, sound, and motion converse in real time, creating immersive environments capable of transporting the viewer into a heightened state of sensory awareness.⁠
⁠
By exploring the poetic potential of generative systems and the sensitivity of human–machine interaction, the studio blurs the boundary between the physical and the digital, transforming space into a field of experimentation open to new forms of storytelling.⁠

🎆 Niu・@sixnfive @sixnfive.lab ⁠
⁠
« Niu », by Six N. Five, is a contemplative lamp holding a digital world within. This light piece is part of an in-depth exploration of how digital elements can be integrated into physical forms.⁠
⁠
Named “Nid”, it evokes a digital shelter where light seems to find refuge and come to life. Rather than being confined to a single surface, the piece radiates its glow through five luminous faces, creating the impression of light breathing and expanding from within. It is not a simple static object, it transforms, responds, and appears almost alive.⁠
⁠
Now on view at @dissenyhub until January 2026.⁠
⁠

Khám phá La Maison de l’Indochine Poker và Échos de l’Histoire Indochinopoly bên trong INDOCHINE COLLECTION – Nhịp sống Đông Dương vượt thời gian.

Échos de l’Histoire Indochinopoly tái hiện nhịp sống rực rỡ của phố thị – nơi từng con đường, nhà ga, khu chợ mang hơi thở của một thời giao thoa văn minh.
Các token gốm thủ công mô phỏng xe hơi cổ và tàu lửa hơi nước – biểu trưng cho khát vọng tiến bộ và tinh thần hiện đại của thời kỳ ấy. Mỗi thẻ Cơ hội, Khí vận như một lát cắt của đời sống kinh tế – xã hội, đưa người chơi trở lại với nhịp sống sôi động của thành thị đầu thế kỷ 20.

La Maison de l’Indochine Poker lại mở ra một nhịp khác – chậm rãi và sâu lắng hơn.
Lấy cảm hứng từ các biệt thự cổ như Bình Thủy, Huỳnh Thủy Lê và Cai Cường – nơi ánh sáng phương Tây len qua khung cửa Á Đông – bộ Poker mang vẻ thanh lịch, ấm áp và đầy hoài niệm. Bề mặt hộp được sơn mài thủ công, làm nổi bật những minh họa tinh tế và đường nét cổ điển đặc trưng. Bộ bài in mỹ thuật trên nền nhựa PVC cao cấp, mang lại độ bền và cảm giác sang trọng khi cầm nắm. Từng quân chip phủ lớp nhũ đồng ánh kim, gợi sắc sáng của những không gian nội thất xa hoa thời Đông Dương. Mỗi quân bài không chỉ là công cụ, mà còn là “điểm chạm” – nơi cảm xúc, văn hóa và sự tinh tế giao hòa.

✨ Tặng lót ly gốm Indochine cho các đơn hàng từ 2 triệu đồng.

The Coolest Brands in Audio Design

From handcrafted speakers to sculptural sound systems, these brands are redefining what great audio looks (and sounds) like. Each one approaches sound as both a technical craft and a design statement - where acoustics, materials, and form come together in perfect balance.

Title Image: @devonojas

1. @oneaway.sound
Makers of beautiful wooden speakers, including one inspired by the vintage Klangfilm cinema speaker. Their designs merge warmth, presence, and craftsmanship in equal measure.

2. @enton.green
Based in The Surrey Hills, England, their stunning customisable A3 speaker is a half-scale reimagining of the Altec Lansing A6 from 1983, part of the legendary “Voice of the Theatre” lineup.

3. @pavilionaudiosystems
Creating custom audio pieces for homes and commercial spaces, inspired by the built-in sound systems of the 60s and 70s that were unapologetically part of the designed environment.

4. @ojas.support by @devonojas
Devon Turnbull’s Ojas systems have become cult objects for audiophiles - large-format, analog-driven setups built for immersive, physical listening experiences.

5. @a.for.ara
High-end sculpted speakers that blur the line between sound and art, described by the brand as “an embrace with the art of high-fidelity sound.”

6. @zanette.hifi
Bespoke high-fidelity loudspeakers made in Brazil, combining technical precision with a strong sense of material craftsmanship.

7. @nospiceaudio
A Bangkok-based maker of bespoke horn-loaded speakers, known for their raw, expressive shapes and deep respect for traditional sound engineering.

Which of these would you have in your space? ⤵️💬

#audiodesign #hifidesign #sounddesign #industrialdesign #listeningculture #modernhifi #audiophiles #audiogear

Soup isn’t just food. It’s nourishment. 🍲
That’s why in Cantonese cooking, instead of boiling, which uses high heat and dilutes flavors, we steam it.

Steaming is gentle. It lets the ingredients release their essence slowly, keeping nutrients intact and flavors concentrated. The result is a soup that’s fuller, richer, and more balance. The kind of comfort that warms you from the inside out.

Você vive procurando uma solução barata, fechada e que não espalhe areia pela casa? 👀
⚠️ Salva esse post e compartilha com quem tem um gatinho!
⠀
📦 PASSO A PASSO DIY – Caixa de areia fechada antissujo:
⠀
1️⃣ Compre uma caixa de armazenamento empilhável com tampa (duas do mesmo tamanho).
2️⃣ Pegue um estilete afiado e corte um quadrado na lateral inferior de cada uma – esse será o "portal" do gato. 🐾
3️⃣ Cole um fundo falso na de baixo (pode ser um pedaço de papelão grosso), para evitar que o gato pule direto pra fora.
4️⃣ Empilhe a segunda caixa (sem cortar o fundo), encaixando perfeitamente. Assim ela serve de "tampa".
5️⃣ Use uma areia de mandioca ultrafina, como a Ma Dong Mei, que não levanta poeira, forma torrões firmes e neutraliza o cheiro. 🌿
⠀
💡 Resultado:
➡️ Menos bagunça
➡️ Mais higiene
➡️ Gato feliz, tutor aliviado! 😻
⠀
📌 Testa aí e me conta se funcionou!
🧼 A casa agradece, e o gato também 🐱
⠀
👀 Curtiu? Marca alguém que precisa ver isso AGORA!
⠀

It used to be so stressful to travel with Mia…
🐯🌎
Then I discovered portable litterboxes.

At the beginning I would use a shoe box cover and some litter that I would take with me in a ziplog bag.

One day I ruptured the ziplog bag while in the plane bathroom. It got punctured with something and I spilled litter everywhere.

I have never cleaned up so much litter from the floor in my life. It took me like 20 minutes to clean that tinny bathroom, doing contortions and sweating my a**.

So stressful…

🎯 O dia em que virei “engenheiro de brinquedos” pro meu gato 😹🛠️

Você já viu um brinquedo deixar 4 gatos hipnotizados ao mesmo tempo? 👀
Pois hoje eu decidi provar que amor de pai de pet também se constrói com tesoura e cola! 💖✂️

⚡️ PASSO A PASSO FELINO — Faça você mesmo 🐾
1️⃣ Encontre garrafas pet dura e charmosa (sabe aquela que todo mundo ama guardar?) 
2️⃣ Pegue um compasso (ou um copo redondo, se for do time improviso 😅) e desenhe vários círculos no topo.
3️⃣ Corte os círculos com cuidado — eles serão os buracos da "toca mágica"! 🕳️
4️⃣ Cubra com papel camurça ou pano de pelúcia
5️⃣ Coloque escovas no fundo do túnel e prepare-se: seu gato vai se tornar um verdadeiro operário do entretenimento! 🧰

🐱 Resultado?
Os 4 gatos aqui de casa vieram um por um “fiscalizar” meu trabalho. No final, o Sortudo tomou posse do brinquedo e declarou território! 😼
Simples, barato e… diversão garantida pro seu felino!

💡 Se curtiu essa ideia, marca alguém que também ama mimar os pets sem gastar muito!
🧡 Vamos espalhar amor, criatividade e papelão por aí!

🔖 Salve esse post pra fazer depois e me marca quando montar o seu!
👇 Conta nos comentários: qual o brinquedo DIY favorito do seu gato?

🌲when I was looking for a new cat trees a few years back, I found they were either extremely expensive or kinda ugly so I made my own cat tree from a fallen down pine tree! Here’s how I did it: 🌲

1. I found the ideal tree! Pine is great bc it smells nice and is strong. I found it in our local woods and dragged it home (and got some funny looks👀)
2. I stripped bark with wood working tools 
3. Sanded it until it was smooth and left it to dry out for a few days 
4. Varnished it with a pet safe paint and to seal up the wood 
5. Assembled it by chopping it up and using screws to attach the parts into a base
6. Decorated it! Added some scratch posts, a bridge and a macrame hanging bed (handmade by me!) 

🌲This has held up so well over the past 3 years and the cats use it everyday!! 🌲

Enter the Intelligent Fry Counter:
✅ Reliable, seamless counting
✅ Frees your team to observe, solve problems, and innovate
✅ Turns uncertainty into confidence
No breakthrough - just a return to what works.
Better data. Better decisions. Better aquaculture.

The Best Biryani Hunt officially begins. 🍛🔥
The founders of 23rd Street Pizza — Vijay Shekar, Raunak & Naga — are on a mission to uncover Bangalore’s best biryani.

Their first stop? Amba Bhavani Mess, a true Basavanagudi gem. A hole-in-the-wall spot where the biryani is legendary and the crowd lines up early for a taste. From the spicy keema vada to the rich liver fry and fiery chilli chicken, every dish here packs serious flavor.

Just one rule — get there early. They usually run out by 1 PM, and you don’t want to miss that first scoop.

You do not need substances to feel incredible at raves. ⁠
⁠
You need a dopamine protocol.⁠
⁠
Most ravers spike hard and crash harder. ⁠
⁠
I stack dopamine in layers. Natural peaks. No party favors. Just biology.⁠
⁠
Here is the Afterglow Protocol:⁠
Morning: Sunlight for 20 minutes. Cold shower after your workout. Tyrosine rich breakfast. Eggs. Avocado. Dark chocolate.⁠
Six hours before doors: L Tyrosine supplement. Nicotine pouch if you choose. Caffeine. Stack your dopamine naturally.⁠
During the festival: Let the music do the heavy lifting. Talk to people. Compliment someone. Human connection releases MORE dopamine than scrolling ever will.⁠
Post rave: No bright lights after 10pm. Earplugs. Eye mask. Sleep. The afterglow continues.⁠
⁠
This protocol works WITH training. Weights for confidence. Cardio for stamina. Glutes because goals.⁠
⁠
My clients are thriving at Lost Lands. Electric Forest. Breakaway. Six hours of pure afterglow. No crash. No death scares.⁠
⁠
You deserve to feel alive without risking your life.⁠
⁠
Comment AWAKEN to work with me. ⁠

🛠 壁に“スラットウォール”つけてみた！

使ったのは「STAND BAR」というDIYパーツ✨
（詳しく見たい人は↓ここ見てみて!）
@aiwa_at_home 

今回は寝室の壁にこのパーツを使って下地を取り付けて
スラットウォールを設置しました☺️

＿＿＿

壁への固定は 石膏ボードピンなので、
大きな穴を開けずに設置できて、原状回復も◎
（賃貸でも安心して使えます🙆‍♀️）

壁に棚を作る時用に使う人が多いと思うんだけど、
こんなふうにスラットウォールの設置にも使えるんです👏

アイデア次第で使い方いろいろで
スタンドバーめちゃくちゃ優秀だなって思いました💕

＿＿＿

今回のDIYでいちばん大変だったのは
「スラットウォールの下地作り」でした😇

というのも…
開閉式にしたかったので上の部分にはスライドレールを
取り付けたい。でも下部分にはつけない、となると、
板の厚みをそろえるのが地味に難しくて💦
スライドレールの位置決めにも苦戦💦

＿＿＿

しかもSTAND BARって、天井ギリギリには設置できないから数センチは空けなきゃいけないことも後から気づいて…
そこも全然計算に入れてなかったから何回も作り直すはめに🌀

正直、取り付け前の下地作りだけでかなり時間かかりました。笑

＿＿＿

でも、ゼロから自分で考えてカタチにできるのはやっぱり楽しい✨

Date night often means eating, drinking, staying up late, and falling asleep before intimacy can happen. 🥺

Use the “F*ck First” rule to increase the likelihood of intimacy. Instead of waiting until the end of the night, prioritize intimacy *before* you go out. 🍷🍽

Think about it—by the time the date’s over, most of us feel full, bloated, tipsy, or just plain tired. And honestly, all you probably want to do is crash into bed, right? 😴

By putting intimacy first, you’ll avoid the post-date slump and create a stronger connection for the rest of the evening. 💫

And share this with your partner so they know what you’ll be up to before your next date night! 😏

In Guizhou, China, even street food comes with a face — literally. Across towns and cities, local vendors proudly hang signs with their own selfies or portraits as the logo. No fake branding or fancy slogans — just “Sister Zhang’s Rice Noodles” with her smiling face printed right above the stall. 😎

This “real-name stall” culture has become a signature of Guizhou’s food scene — honest, confident, and refreshingly human. When the boss’s photo is the brand, customers instantly know who’s behind their bowl of noodles 🍜. Some even stick their Douyin or WeChat QR codes right next to the sign, inviting people to follow or message them directly.

And if you think this idea sounds familiar — look at Laoganma, the legendary chili sauce brand from Guizhou. Its founder Tao Huabi’s serious portrait is one of China’s most recognizable food logos — and a perfect example of this “my face, my brand” confidence 🔥.

Netizens say it best: “When your selfie is the logo, you’d better cook like your reputation depends on it.” In a world full of faceless chains, Guizhou’s selfie stalls bring back something rare — authenticity, trust, and pure human flavor. ❤️‍🔥

I never free-feed my cats or leave a bowl around. Instead, I turn mealtime into a foraging adventure and it’s amazing for their mind, body, and instincts!

Indoor cats often miss out on the mental and physical stimulation they’d get in the wild. That’s where foraging games come in—turning mealtime or playtime into a mini hunting adventure!

✨ Why foraging is important:
👉Provides mental stimulation, keeping your cat sharp and curious.
👉Encourages natural hunting behaviors, satisfying instincts that indoor life can’t always provide.
👉Helps prevent boredom and behavior problems, like over-grooming or excessive meowing.

🎯 Simple ways to add foraging:
1️⃣ Scatter your cat’s food around a puzzle feeder or box.
2️⃣ Hide treats in puzzles, small cups or under objects for them to find.
3️⃣ Rotate toys and foraging setups to keep it novel and exciting.

💡 Your cat will be mentally stimulated, physically active, and having fun—all while tapping into instincts they were born with!

💾 save this and Share so you won’t forget!

‼️THIS IS IN KARNATAKA‼️

Not Bali. Not Thailand.
This is Tanmatrra Ecoluxe Resort, an eco-luxury retreat in the heart of the Western Ghats, Mudigere. Just a 5–6 hour drive from Bangalore, it is a hidden gem where sustainability and comfort come together to create an unforgettable getaway.

What makes Tanmatrra truly special is its attention to detail. The stay itself is an experience—rammed-earth cottages built to stay naturally cool and cozy, furnished with reclaimed wood, and finished with natural stone. Even the bathrooms stand out, featuring open-air showers that let you bathe under the sky, paired with natural handmade soaps that add to the earthy luxury.

Mornings here begin with mist-kissed mountains, the sound of birds, and the sight of a private stream flowing right through the property. Days can be as peaceful or adventurous as you want them to be—enjoy quiet walks by the stream, sit by a campfire in the evening, or set out on treks along scenic forest trails. For the adventurous, jeep rides take you to hidden private waterfalls, offering an experience that feels untouched and exclusive.

The food at Tanmatrra is another highlight. You are served authentic Malenadu cuisine, prepared with locally sourced ingredients and rich in traditional flavors. A must-try is their Sudubutti, a rare dish slow-cooked in banana leaves over charcoal, a cooking style that reflects the region’s roots and traditions.

Every element here is designed to bring you closer to nature without compromising comfort—making it perfect for a romantic escape, a family getaway, or simply some much-needed time away from the city.

Location: Mudigere, Western Ghats
Travel time: Approximately 5–6 hours from Bangalore

If you are looking for an eco-conscious, luxurious yet serene escape, Tanmatrra Ecoluxe Resort is the place you’ve been searching for.

Choosing the right exhaust fan 🪭 ? Focus on CFM (Cubic Feet per Minute) — it tells you how much air the fan moves per minute!

💡Quick guide:

✅Bathroom (under 100 sq.ft) ➔ 1 CFM per sq.ft. (e.g., 60 sq.ft = 60 CFM)

✅Large Bathroom (>100 sq.ft) ➔ Add 50 CFM for each toilet, shower, and tub.

✅Kitchen ➔ 15–20 air changes/hour = around 300–400 CFM for an average kitchen.

✅Laundry Room ➔ Around 100–120 CFM needed.

💡Tip:

✅Always round up your CFM for better airflow.

✅Lower "sones" = quieter fan.

✅Bigger duct = smoother airflow.

✅Ventilation = better health + longer life for your home!

തമിഴ്നാട് സംസ്ഥാനത്തിലെ കൊടൈക്കനാലിൽ നിന്നും 40 കിലോമീറ്റർ ദൂരെയാണ് കൂക്കൽ ഗ്രാമം സ്ഥിതിചെയ്യുന്നത്. 
സമുദ്രനിരപ്പിൽ നിന്നും 6200 അടി ഉയരത്തിലാണ് കുക്കൽ സ്ഥിതിചെയ്യുന്നത്.

ട്രെക്കിങ്ങ് ഇഷ്ടമുള്ളവർക്ക് ഇവിടെ കൂക്കൾ ഗുഹ സന്ദർശിക്കാം.കൂക്കൽ ഗുഹകൾ,പുരാതന ശിലാപാഠശാലകളാണ് , അവർ ഇലവസ്ത്രം ധരിച്ചിരുന്ന യഥാർത്ഥ പാലയാർ (പഴയവർ) എന്നർത്ഥം ഗോത്രങ്ങളുടെ പിൻഗാമികൾ താമസിച്ചിരുന്നതായി വിശ്വസിക്കപ്പെടുന്നു . 

ഇവിടെ കാണാനായി നിരയായി ചെയ്യുന്ന ടെറസ് കൃഷി, വെള്ളച്ചാട്ടങ്ങൾ,  തടാകങ്ങൾ എന്നിവ കാണ
തടാകതിൻ്റെ 3 വശവും പർവതങ്ങളാൽ ചുറ്റപ്പെട്ടിരിക്കുന്നു. ഗ്രാമത്തിൽ തന്നെ ഗൈഡുകൾ ലഭ്യമാണ്, അവർ നിങ്ങളെ കുന്നുകളിലെ മനോഹരമായ ചില സ്ഥലങ്ങളിലേക്ക് കൊണ്ടുപോകും.

കൂക്കലിൽ വളരെ കുറച്ച് താമസ സൗകര്യങ്ങളെ ഉള്ളൂ അതിനാൽ മുൻകൂട്ടി സ്റ്റേ ബുക്ക് ചെയ്തിട്ട് വേണം അങ്ങോട്ട് പോവാൻ.

For everyone confused, the visa is valid for 5 years with multiple entries, allowing a maximum stay of 90 days per visit. It’s not 90 days in total over 5 years.

Quick Submission Process (regardless of city):
	1.	Select Temporary Visitor (Tourism) visa.
	2.	Download the official MOFA Japan application form, fill neatly, paste 45 × 35 mm photo.
	3.	Gather documents: passport, form, photo, flight + hotel bookings, itinerary, bank statements (3 mo) & ITR, proof of work/income, cover letter, ID/address proof.
	4.	Submit to Consulate (Mumbai) or your local VFS centre (walk-in allowed in listed cities).
	5.	Pay visa + VFS fees, get receipt with tracking ID.
	6.	Track application online—processing usually 4–10 working days.
	7.	Upon approval: receive eVisa or stamped passport. Check visa validity, carry itinerary & travel insurance.

🚀 P.S. If you want to learn how to start planning your bathroom renovation (even if you have zero experience)… you’ll love our FREE live training event “The Ultimate Starter Guide to Bathroom Renovations”!

💬 Comment “START” to secure your seat now!

A Luxury look doesn’t have to be expensive!! Small details can transform your bathroom into a luxurious space, these are 7 subtle yet impactful details! ✨🚿

1️⃣ Textured Tiles: Add depth and visual interest with beautifully textured tiles. 

2️⃣ Separate Shower Head & Handheld: Enjoy the flexibility and convenience of a separate shower head and handheld unit while cleaning up the wall space visually🖐️

3️⃣ Tiled Window Reveals: Elevate the elegance by tiling the window reveals for a seamless look. 

4️⃣ Venetian Plaster/Microcement: Opt for venetian plaster or microcement for a sleek, modern finish. 

5️⃣ In-Wall Toilet: Save space and create a clean look with an in-wall toilet.

6️⃣ Mitred Edges: Achieve a polished finish with perfectly mitred tile edges. 

7️⃣ Tiles Flush to Door Frame: tile flush to the door frame for a continuous and sophisticated design. 🚪

Antique Mirror Tile Backsplash AND Secret Slide Out Shelves — which is better??

This tile is Floor & Decor’s Viviano Antique Mirror Glass Tile. The beveled edges reflect light in the very best way!

I used MusselBound on the wall first in place of mortar — it’s basically really strong double stick tape that works beautifully in dry locations for glass, porcelain, and ceramic tile! I usually prefer mortar when working with real stone!

I started in the center and worked my way out, checking levelness as I went and installing each side by side. I decided to vertically stack these and love the look!

I did get a special glass tile cutting blade for my wet saw and had to go slowly with each cut — it’s pretty prone to chipping and breaking & I didn’t want to grout these!

For the shelves…

The trick is for each shelf you want to cut identical boards for the top and bottom! That way, the bottom board covered the slides. Nail the top board into your shelf braces. Install your slides to the shelf braces on the sides underneath, then measure your width between the slides. This is the width your drawer sides need to be (the 1x2s).

Use your second shelf board as the bottom and install 1x2s on top of it according to your width measurement to act as your drawer sides and back.

Attach it to your slides and use a 3” board on the front — only nailing into the bottom and sides, not the top!

That way, when it’s closed, it just looks like a solid shelf! You can hide all kinds of things in here ✨

Oh and the brass rails? LAMP PARTS! I have a blog post tutorial on this here — https://farmhouseishblog.com/diy-brass-rails/

Are these not the perfect finishing touches?? I can’t wait to decorate and fill this up!!

Which is your fav — the backspash or the shelves?

In the North of Vietnam people usually say Cơm Rang (pronounced kind of like “kuhm zahng”). In the South people often say Cơm Chiên (pronounced like “kuhm chee-en”). Both mean fried rice, but if you are traveling it is good to know the difference so you can order it anywhere in the country.

Fried rice, is loved all across Asia. Here it usually comes with the rice and the toppings separate, so you mix it yourself at the table. Kind of DIY fried rice.
