How we managed the wiring for a sliding TV in front of a full-wall wardrobe!

Our client needed maximum storage in the bedroom, so we converted one wall into an L-shaped wardrobe (8 ft high + loft). But they also wanted their TV on the same wall.

The solution? A sliding TV panel mounted in front of the wardrobe shutters, so the TV can glide left ↔️ right and the shutters can still be accessed easily.

But here’s the tricky part—the wiring! 
Here’s how we solved it:
1️⃣ We created a 2.5 inch gap between the wardrobe and loft to pass the TV wires neatly from the back wall.
2️⃣ From this gap, the wires were routed to the sliding panel.
3️⃣ On the back of the sliding panel, the wires were clipped and pinned securely—so when the panel slides, the wires move seamlessly without tangling.
4️⃣ On the TV panel itself, we provided easy-access points for TV connections, along with an HDMI cable left ready in case the client wants to connect Tata Sky / Dish TV in the future.
5️⃣ Within the 2.5 inch gap, we left a narrow openable access point, so that if there’s ever an issue with the wiring, it can be easily maintained and repaired.

This way, the client got:
✔️ Maximum storage
✔️ A neat sliding TV unit
✔️ Future-proof wiring solutions
✔️ Zero visible mess of wires

I first booked a very random place in Hampi for the night and was left so disappointed. ☹️ Then, I remembered there is a Zostel somewhere around and immediately knew what to do- I put it on google maps, it was some 20 kms away! So I rented a scooty the next day, loaded my bag and left! Uff- BEST DECISION!🌸

✨The property has its own story - everyone in the climbing (boulders) community knows of Sharmila aunty and her three sons, from the Goan Corner, an old establishment in the Hampi Island that was demolished alongside 18 others in March 2020. While they were left homeless for 3 months, they knew they have to start again…bigger, better…which made them purchase this land, away from everything, and start afresh. What you see here is years of hard work, resilience and a zeal that brought the Goan Corner back to life and they continue to host thousands of people every year and keep them happy.

They also have a deep love for animals, gardening, sustainable architecture and it shows! There’s also bunnies, turtles, fish and monitor lizards!!🦎 

And yes they’re under the Zostel umbrella but the place is FULLY family run. It’s amazing to be around them, the way they treat their guests and help them, the way the staff takes care of you and the way the ANIMALSSSSS, alll of them, make sure to let you not be sad even for a second!! it’s amazing 🥰🥰🥰

If you ever find yourself planning a trip to Hampi, book Zostel Hampi to have the best time! 

🪐 I met such lovely souls from all over, spent time with them, swam in the pond, doodled, went on scooty rides, watched movies in the dining hall and spent SO MUCH TIME IN THE LAP OF NATURE 🍃🌱

📅 Hampi’s season time is September to February and post that; it’s toooo hot to do anything! So plan a trip soon 🫶🏻 

Have a beautiful trip- don’t be obsessed with sightseeing. Take everyday as it comes, in Hampi🧡

PS. If you don’t like animals, then don’t go here. Don’t expect them to accommodate their pets as per your liking.

"STOP! Don’t use the SIM800L before knowing these 5 things!"

1. Power Supply is Crucial
SIM800L needs a stable 3.7V–4.2V with at least 2A current. Never power it directly from Arduino 5V or USB — it'll crash or reboot.

2. Use Level Shifters
TX/RX pins are not 5V tolerant. Use a resistor divider or logic level converter to avoid damaging the module.

3. Antenna Matters
Always connect a proper antenna. Without it, you'll struggle with weak signal or failed SMS/calls.

4. Check Network Band
Ensure your SIM card supports 2G network, and your area still has 2G coverage. (Jio doesn't work!)

5. Command Patience
After sending an AT command, wait for the OK before sending the next. Flooding it with commands will cause failure.

Udupi Sri Krishna Bhavan, Balepete

Located right in the heart of Balepete, Udupi Sri Krishna Bhavan has been serving authentic South Indian flavors for almost a century. 

The place feels like stepping back into old Bengaluru — simple wooden chairs, the buzz of regulars.

Famous for their Sagu Masala Dosa & Badam Halwa.

Where? Janardhan Buildings,
No.123-124, Balepete,
Bangalore-560053

Timings: 8 AM – 10 PM (Mon–Fri), 7:30 AM – 10 PM (Sat–Sun) 

*typo error in the video, this iconic place is located in the Balepete.

** Update on our Volunteer Program ⤵️
 Thanks so much everyone for the amazing response to our volunteering opportunity! We had wonderful volunteers join us in September and October and we truly appreciate everyone who applied or showed the interest.

For now, the volunteer program is on pause as we do not require new volunteers for the upcoming months. We’ll share an update on our stories and posts, if we reopen the program in the future.

If you still would like to try surfing and to be part of the Mulki vibe, you’re always welcome to join us for our surf courses or other activities at our camp anytime🤙🏽 Thank you for all the love and Sea you in Mulki! 🌊🏄‍♀️🏄‍♂️
_____________________________

We’re looking for volunteers🏄‍♂️🏄‍♀️

We’re inviting passionate people to join our surf community as volunteers.

✨ What you’ll do:
▪️ Conduct soft-skills training for our staff (3–4 hrs/day, 5-7 days) 

✨ What you get:
🏄‍♂️ Unlimited access to surfing, kayaking & more
🌮 Comfortable stay + delicious food
🌍 A chance to be a part of our surf community in Mulki and connect with amazing people from all over India.

📅 Upcoming Dates
Open for September and October months. 
Volunteer opportunities for November & December will be announced in mid-October (if required). 

The volunteering program is open to our surf fam, so make sure you’re following us to get the application form 🤙

If this sounds like you → Follow @mulki.in and Drop “I’m in” in the comments to Enroll 🤙🏽

This is such a good find.. Details below 👇 

Most people in Bengaluru don’t even know this exists — but there’s a 100-year-old furniture factory right inside the city where architects and designers have been sourcing their premium pieces for decades.

Here, you don’t just buy furniture. You sit with a designer, finalize every detail, pick your wood — whether it’s Burma teak, rosewood, or regular teak — and then watch as raw timber gets cut, steam dried, planed, joined, sanded, and polished into something timeless.

The best part? You see the entire journey of your piece, from concept to craft. And with their studio showcasing beautiful finished pieces, kitchens, and wardrobes, you get both inspiration and confidence in the quality.

This isn’t just shopping — it’s creating something that’s truly yours.
